description: Three interdependent PHP repos (a shared domain library and two services that consume it) used to verify cross-repository dependency, DI wiring & coupling analysis (`crossref`). Framework-free so it runs with no composer/vendor.

repos.name: shared
repos.role: domain library (Shared\*), incl. namespaced function Shared\Util\money_round

repos.name: web
repos.role: service, depends on shared (Web\*), incl. classless bootstrap.php

conventions.cross_repo_refs: expected CrossRepoRef edges (Class -> Class in another repo), deduped by (source_repo, target_repo, target_fqcn, kind). kind: import (use statement, attributed to the classes that mention the alias) | class_ref (::class literal / callable string) | extends / implements / uses (heritage) | new (instantiation) | type_hint (parameter or return type) | instanceof.
conventions.cross_repo_file_refs: expected CrossRepoFileRef edges (File -> Class in another repo) for files that define NO classes (bootstrap/config wiring); same kinds as cross_repo_refs, deduped by (source_repo, target_repo, target_fqcn, kind).
conventions.cross_repo_calls: expected CrossRepoCalls edges (Method -> Method in another repo), by (source_repo, target_repo, target_fqmn). Receiver types come from typed parameters, constructor-injected (promoted) properties, and chained receivers whose type is recovered from an earlier resolved call's declared return type. Plain namespaced-function calls also land here ((global) pseudo-class endpoints).
conventions.cross_repo_binds: expected DiBinds edges from `services()` `add(Interface::class, Concrete::class)` — (source_repo=interface's repo, target_repo=concrete's repo, symbol=concrete fqcn).
conventions.cross_repo_injects: expected DiInjects edges from `services()` `add(Service::class)->addArgument(Dep::class)` — (source_repo=service's repo, target_repo=dep's repo, symbol=dep fqcn).
conventions.coupling: expected repo->repo counts: refs=CrossRepoRef+CrossRepoFileRef, calls=CrossRepoCalls, di=DiBinds+DiInjects.
conventions.expected_drops: everything the derivation must refuse to edge AND must report: `ambiguous` (fqcn -> repos defining it) and `unowned_sample` (referenced FQCNs owned by no ingested repo).
conventions.exactness: the derivation must produce EXACTLY these edges — no more (negative controls below must NOT appear).

cross_repo_refs.from: Web\Time\SystemClock (via use alias) and Web\Time\FrozenClock (inline FQCN, no import)

cross_repo_refs_note: Exact-set tests key on (source_repo, target_repo, target_fqcn, kind); distinct source classes yield distinct rows, so (Price, type_hint) covers OrderService AND Cart, and (Clock, implements) covers SystemClock AND FrozenClock — 13 web + 4 api = 17 CrossRepoRef rows total.

cross_repo_calls.why: chained receiver: $sum = $base->add($tax) types $sum as Price via add()'s declared return type
namespaced function called by FQCN from web/bootstrap.php web_boot()

cross_repo_binds.why: Web\Application::services() binds Clock interface to SystemClock via add(Clock::class, SystemClock::class)

cross_repo_injects.why: Web\Application::services() injects the shared Logger into OrderService via addArgument(Logger::class)

negative_controls.detail: Web\Support\Logger shares the simple name 'Logger' with Shared\Logging\Logger. Web\Support\LocalReporter uses the LOCAL one (unqualified name in the same namespace — PHP class names never fall back to other namespaces), so it must NOT produce any cross-repo edge to shared.
Web\Order\DynamicDispatcher::run calls $handler->handle() on a runtime-computed receiver; no cross-repo edge (documented method-level boundary).
Web\Application type-hints Cake\Core\ContainerInterface, which no ingested repo owns — it must appear in drops.unowned_sample and never as an edge.

Chained receivers are only typed through methods with DECLARED return types; a chain through an untyped method stays uncaptured (remaining honest boundary — Price::add declares `: Price`, which is why Price::amount IS captured above).
